fact: Sachin Tendulkar has scored the most runs in international cricket, with over 34,000 runs and a record 100 international centuries.
In December 2021, New Zealand spinner Ajaz Patel became only the third bowler in Test cricket history to take all 10 wickets in a single innings, achieving this feat against India in Mumbai.
The first official international cricket match was played in September 1844 between the United States and Canada at Bloomingdale Park in New York.
Lionel Messi has won a record eight Ballon d'Or awards, the most by any player in history, with his eighth award coming in October 2023.
The FIFA World Cup in 2022 was hosted by Qatar and won by Argentina, who defeated France on penalties in the final after a 3-3 draw.
Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record for the most international goals scored by an individual player in men's football history, surpassing Ali Daei's long-standing record of 109 goals.
Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam men's singles titles in history, winning his 24th title at the US Open in September 2023.
Rafael Nadal is known as the 'King of Clay' and has won the French Open (Roland Garros) a record 14 times, losing only 3 matches in the tournament over his career.
Serena Williams has won 23 Grand Slam singles titles in the Open Era, the most by any player (male or female) in the Open Era, and second all-time behind Margaret Court's 24.

Lin Dan of China is widely considered one of the greatest badminton players of all time and is the first and only player to complete the 'Super Grand Slam' by winning all nine major titles in world badminton.
The Thomas Cup is the premier international men's team badminton championship, held every two years, and India won it for the first time in 2022 by defeating Indonesia in the final.
An Se-young of South Korea won the women's singles gold medal at the BWF World Championships in August 2023, becoming the first South Korean to win that category.
LeBron James became the all-time leading scorer in NBA history in February 2023, surpassing Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's long-standing record of 38,387 points.
The Boston Celtics and the Los Angeles Lakers are tied for the most NBA championships in history, with each franchise having won 17 titles.
The USA Men's Basketball Team won the gold medal at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics (held in 2021) by defeating France in the final match.
